entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary crop studied for drought tolerance in West Africa

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: The primary environmental stressor limiting rice production

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where rainfed upland rice production is limited by drought

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The specific region where rice genotypes were studied

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Factor that aggravates drought stress in Africa

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The threat caused by limited rice production due to drought

entities.name: MORPHO-PHYSIOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Physical and functional characteristics of rice genotypes

entities.name: BIOCHEMICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Chemical characteristics of rice genotypes

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per plant or area

entities.name: YIELD-RELATED TRAITS
entities.description: Traits that contribute to the overall grain yield

entities.name: RELATIVE WATER CONTENT
entities.description: A biochemical trait used to monitor drought tolerance

entities.name: MALONDIALDEHYDE
entities.description: A biochemical marker of oxidative stress used to monitor drought tolerance

entities.name: PROLINE
entities.description: A biochemical trait collected during drought screening

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY PARAMETERS
entities.description: Parameters used to quantify genetic variability among genotypes

entities.name: SPIKELET FERTILITY
entities.description: A yield-related trait with high heritability under drought stress

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: A yield-related trait with high heritability under drought stress

entities.name: HUNDRED GRAIN WEIGHT
entities.description: A yield-related trait with high heritability under drought stress

entities.name: LEAF ROLLING SCORE
entities.description: A morpho-physiological trait with high heritability under drought stress

entities.name: LEAF DRYING SCORE
entities.description: A morpho-physiological trait with high heritability under drought stress

entities.name: GRAIN LENGTH
entities.description: A trait recommended for monitoring drought tolerance

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice genotypes to maintain productivity under drought stress

entities.name: ALPHA-LATTICE DESIGN
entities.description: Experimental design used for field screening of 100 genotypes

entities.name: COMPLETELY RANDOMIZED BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: Experimental design used for greenhouse screening of 14 genotypes

entities.name: CORRELATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: Statistical method used to recommend traits for monitoring drought tolerance

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: Statistical method used to recommend traits for monitoring drought tolerance

entities.name: MULTI-TRAIT GENOTYPE-IDEOTYPE DISTANCE INDEX
entities.description: Index used to recommend traits for monitoring drought tolerance

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Drought stress occurring during the reproductive stage of the crop

entities.name: NON-STRESS CONDITIONS
entities.description: Control conditions without drought stress

entities.name: UPLAND FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environment where initial field screening was conducted

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment where secondary screening was conducted

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The diversity in genetic makeup among the rice genotypes

entities.name: BROAD-SENSE HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variance due to genetic factors

entities.name: GENETIC ADVANCE
entities.description: The expected genetic gain from selection

entities.name: GLABERRIMA ECOTYPE
entities.description: A specific ecotype of rice, exemplified by genotype G60

entities.name: INDICA ECOTYPE
entities.description: A specific ecotype of rice, exemplified by genotypes G100, G99, and G11
